children.heading: Status of Corporation and corporate powers; obligations not federally guaranteed
children.label: a Status of Corporation and corporate powers; obligations not federally guaranteed

children.heading: Status of the Corporation
children.label: 1 Status of the Corporation
children.text: The Corporation shall not be an agency, instrumentality, or establishment of the United States Government, nor a Government corporation, nor a Government controlled corporation, as such terms are defined in . No action under (commonly known as the Tucker Act) shall be allowable against the United States based on the actions of the Corporation.section 103 of title 5section 1491 of title 28

children.heading: Corporate powers
children.label: 2 Corporate powers
children.text: The Corporation shall be subject to the provisions of this section, and, to the extent not inconsistent with this section, to the District of Columbia Business Corporation Act (or the comparable law of another State, if applicable). The Corporation shall have the powers conferred upon a corporation by the District of Columbia Business Corporation Act (or such other applicable State law) as from time to time in effect in order to conduct the Corporation’s affairs as a private, for-profit corporation and to carry out the Corporation’s purposes and activities incidental thereto. The Corporation shall have the power to enter into contracts, to execute instruments, to incur liabilities, to provide products and services, and to do all things as are necessary or incidental to the proper management of the Corporation’s affairs and the efficient operation of a private, for-profit business.

children.heading: Student Loan Marketing Association
children.label: A Student Loan Marketing Association
children.text: The Student Loan Marketing Association shall not increase its share of the ownership of the Corporation in excess of 42 percent of the shares of stock of the Corporation outstanding on . The Student Loan Marketing Association shall not control the operation of the Corporation, except that the Student Loan Marketing Association may participate in the election of directors as a shareholder, and may continue to exercise the Student Loan Marketing Association’s right to appoint directors under as long as that section is in effect.September 30, 1996section 1132f–3 of this title

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: B Prohibition
children.text: Until such time as the Secretary of the Treasury sells the stock of the Corporation owned by the Secretary of Education pursuant to subsection (c), the Student Loan Marketing Association shall not provide financial support or guarantees to the Corporation.

children.heading: Financial support or guarantees
children.label: C Financial support or guarantees
children.text: After the Secretary of the Treasury sells the stock of the Corporation owned by the Secretary of Education pursuant to subsection (c), the Student Loan Marketing Association may provide financial support or guarantees to the Corporation, if such support or guarantees are subject to terms and conditions that are no more advantageous to the Corporation than the terms and conditions the Student Loan Marketing Association provides to other entities, including, where applicable, other monoline financial guaranty corporations in which the Student Loan Marketing Association has no ownership interest.

children.heading: Obligations insured by the Corporation
children.label: A Obligations insured by the Corporation

children.heading: Full faith and credit of the United States
children.label: i Full faith and credit of the United States
children.text: No obligation that is insured, guaranteed, or otherwise backed by the Corporation shall be deemed to be an obligation that is guaranteed by the full faith and credit of the United States.

children.heading: Student Loan Marketing Association
children.label: ii Student Loan Marketing Association
children.text: No obligation that is insured, guaranteed, or otherwise backed by the Corporation shall be deemed to be an obligation that is guaranteed by the Student Loan Marketing Association.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: iii Special rule
children.text: This paragraph shall not affect the determination of whether such obligation is guaranteed for purposes of Federal income taxes.

children.heading: Securities offered by the Corporation
children.label: B Securities offered by the Corporation
children.text: No debt or equity securities of the Corporation shall be deemed to be guaranteed by the full faith and credit of the United States.

children.heading: “Corporation” defined
children.label: 5 “Corporation” defined
children.text: The term “Corporation” as used in this section means the College Construction Loan Insurance Association as in existence on the day before , and any successor corporation.September 30, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: such obligations or such securities, as the case may be, are not obligations of the United States, nor are such obligations or such securities, as the case may be, guaranteed in any way by the full faith and credit of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Corporation is not an instrumentality of the United States.

children.heading: Additional notice
children.label: B Additional notice
children.text: During the five-year period following the sale of stock pursuant to subsection (c)(1), in addition to the notice requirements in subparagraph (A), the Corporation shall include, in each of the contracts and documents referred to in such subparagraph, a prominent statement providing notice that the United States is not an investor in the Corporation.

children.heading: Corporate charter
children.label: 2 Corporate charter
children.text: The Corporation’s charter shall be amended as necessary and without delay to conform to the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Corporate name
children.label: 3 Corporate name
children.text: The name of the Corporation, or of any direct or indirect subsidiary thereof, may not contain the term “College Construction Loan Insurance Association”, or any substantially similar variation thereof.

children.heading: Articles of incorporation
children.label: 4 Articles of incorporation
children.text: The Corporation shall amend the Corporation’s articles of incorporation without delay to reflect that one of the purposes of the Corporation shall be to guarantee, insure, and reinsure bonds, leases, and other evidences of debt of educational institutions, including Historically Black Colleges and Universities and other academic institutions which are ranked in the lower investment grade category using a nationally recognized credit rating system.

children.heading: Requirements until stock sale
children.label: 5 Requirements until stock sale
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (d), the requirements of sections 1132f–3 and 1132f–9 of this title, as such sections were in effect on the day before , shall continue to be effective until the day immediately following the date of closing of the purchase of the Secretary of Education’s stock (or the date of closing of the final purchase, in the case of multiple transactions) pursuant to subsection (c)(1) of this Act.September 30, 19961So in original. Probably should be “section.”1

children.heading: Sale of federally owned stock
children.label: c Sale of federally owned stock

children.heading: Purchase by the Corporation
children.label: 1 Purchase by the Corporation
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall sell and the Corporation shall purchase, within 90 days after , the stock of the Corporation held by the Secretary of Education at a price determined by the binding, independent appraisal of a nationally recognized financial firm, except that the 90-day period may be extended by mutual agreement of the Secretary of the Treasury and the Corporation to not more than 150 days after . The appraiser shall be jointly selected by the Secretary of the Treasury and the Corporation. In the event that the Secretary of the Treasury and the Corporation cannot agree on the appraiser, then the Secretary of the Treasury and the Corporation shall name an independent third party to select the appraiser.September 30, 1996September 30, 1996

children.heading: Reimbursement of costs and expenses of sale
children.label: 2 Reimbursement of costs and expenses of sale
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall be reimbursed from the proceeds of the sale of the stock under this subsection for all reasonable costs and expenses related to such sale, except that one-half of all reasonable costs and expenses relating to the independent appraisal under paragraph (1) shall be borne by the Corporation.

children.heading: Deposit into account
children.label: 3 Deposit into account
children.text: Amounts collected from the sale of stock pursuant to this subsection that are not used to reimburse the Secretary of the Treasury pursuant to paragraph (2) shall be deposited into the account established under subsection (e).

children.heading: Assistance by the Corporation
children.label: 4 Assistance by the Corporation
children.text: The Corporation shall provide such assistance as the Secretary of the Treasury and the Secretary of Education may require to facilitate the sale of the stock under this subsection.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 5 Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Secretary of the Treasury shall report to the appropriate committees of Congress on the completion and terms of the sale of stock of the Corporation pursuant to this subsection.September 30, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: amounts collected from the sale and proceeds resulting from the exercise of stock warrants pursuant to ;section 1087–3(c)(9) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: amounts and proceeds remitted as compensation for the right to assign the “Sallie Mae” name as a trademark or service mark pursuant to ; andsection 1087–3(e)(3) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: amounts and proceeds collected from the sale of the stock of the Corporation and deposited pursuant to subsection (c)(3).

children.heading: Amounts and proceeds relating to Sallie Mae
children.label: A Amounts and proceeds relating to Sallie Mae
children.text: The amounts and proceeds described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) shall be used to finance public elementary and secondary school facility construction and repair within the District of Columbia or to carry out the District of Columbia School Reform Act of 1995.

children.heading: Amounts and proceeds relating to Connie Lee
children.label: B Amounts and proceeds relating to Connie Lee
children.text: The amounts and proceeds described in subparagraph (C) of paragraph (1) shall be used to finance public and public charter elementary and secondary school facility construction and repair within the District of Columbia. Of such amounts and proceeds, $5,000,000 shall be set aside for a credit enhancement revolving fund for public charter schools in the District of Columbia, to be administered and disbursed in accordance with paragraph (3).

children.heading: Credit enhancement revolving fund for public charter schools
children.label: 3 Credit enhancement revolving fund for public charter schools
i
children.text: 50 percent shall be used to make grants under subparagraph (B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: 50 percent shall be used to make grants under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Grants to eligible nonprofit corporations
children.label: B Grants to eligible nonprofit corporations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Using the amounts described in subparagraph (A)(i), the Mayor of the District of Columbia shall make and disburse grants to eligible nonprofit corporations to carry out the purposes described in subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Administration
children.label: ii Administration
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (F), the Mayor shall administer the program of grants under this subparagraph, except that if the committee described in subparagraph (C)(iii) is in operation and is fully functional prior to the date the Mayor makes the grants, the Mayor may delegate the administration of the program to the committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Using the amounts described in subparagraph (A)(ii), the Mayor of the District of Columbia shall make grants to entities to carry out the purposes described in subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Participation of schools
children.label: ii Participation of schools
children.text: A public charter school in the District of Columbia may receive a grant under this subparagraph to carry out the purposes described in subparagraph (E) in the same manner as other entities receiving grants to carry out such activities.

children.heading: Administration through committee
children.label: iii Administration through committee
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (F), the Mayor shall carry out this subparagraph through the committee appointed by the Mayor under the second sentence of paragraph (2)(B) (as in effect prior to ). The committee may enter into an agreement with a third party to carry out its responsibilities under this subparagraph.November 22, 2000

children.heading: Cap on administrative costs
children.label: iv Cap on administrative costs
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the funds available for grants under this subparagraph for a fiscal year may be used to cover the administrative costs of making grants under this subparagraph for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Special rule regarding eligibility of nonprofit corporations
children.label: D Special rule regarding eligibility of nonprofit corporations
children.text: In order to be eligible to receive a grant under this paragraph, a nonprofit corporation must provide appropriate certification to the Mayor or to the committee described in subparagraph (C)(iii) (as the case may be) that it is duly authorized by two or more public charter schools in the District of Columbia to act on their behalf in obtaining financing (or in assisting them in obtaining financing) to cover the costs of activities described in subparagraph (E)(i).

children.label: I
children.text: obtaining financing to acquire interests in real property (including by purchase, lease, or donation), including financing to cover planning, development, and other incidental costs;

children.label: II
children.text: obtaining financing for construction of facilities or the renovation, repair, or alteration of existing property or facilities (including the purchase or replacement of fixtures and equipment), including financing to cover planning, development, and other incidental costs;

children.label: III
children.text: enhancing the availability of loans (including mortgages) and bonds; and

children.label: IV
children.text: obtaining lease guarantees (in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Office of Public Charter School Financing).

children.heading: No direct funding for schools
children.label: ii No direct funding for schools
children.text: Funds provided under a grant under this subparagraph may not be used by a recipient to make direct loans or grants to public charter schools.

children.heading: Role of Office of Public Charter School Financing and Support
children.label: F Role of Office of Public Charter School Financing and Support
children.text: During fiscal year 2003 and each succeeding fiscal year, the Office of Public Charter School Financing and Support shall be responsible for receiving applications, making payments, and otherwise administering this paragraph, except that no grant may be made under this paragraph without the approval of the committee described in subparagraph (C)(iii).
